item.title: Canada Eyes Free Trade Pacts With Philippines, Asean This Year
item.description: Canada expects to conclude free trade agreements with the Philippines and the wider Southeast Asian bloc this year, as Ottawa seeks to boost business ties with the region and grow its non-US trade.

item.title: Qatar Asks Ships to Go Dark at LNG Port in New Safety Measure
item.description: Qatar is asking ships at its main liquefied natural gas export facility to turn off their transponders, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Telenor Sells Stake in Internet-of-Things Business to Verdane
item.description: Telenor ASA agreed to sell a stake in its internet-of-things venture to Verdane Group as the telecommunications company overhauls operations to focus on the core Nordic business.

item.title: Mitsubishi Heavy Sees Global Gas Turbine Demand Staying Strong
item.description: Global orders for gas turbines will “decline slightly” this year from 2025, but remain strong due to the build-out of data centers, according to Mitsubishi Heavy Industries Ltd., a major manufacturer of the equipment used in power plants.

item.title: Shiseido May Swap Oil for Plants on Cosmetic Supply Chain Chaos
item.description: Shiseido Co. is looking to potentially swap out oil-based inputs for plant-derived materials as the Middle East conflict upends the global skincare and cosmetics supply chain.

item.title: China Earns $500 Million an Hour From Exports Supercharged by AI
item.description: The US and Chinese economies may still be on their way to decoupling but both are drawing strength from the same source.

item.title: ECB Must Act If Iran War Threatens Price Stability, Nagel Says
item.description: The European Central Bank will continue analyzing the economy before deciding next month whether to raise interest rates, but policymakers must take action if the Iran war jeopardizes price stability, according to Governing Council member Joachim Nagel.

item.title: Russia Cuts Growth Outlook Despite Putin’s Push to Rally Economy
item.description: Russia cut its official growth forecast for this year, even after President Vladimir Putin signaled frustration with the slowdown and ordered the government to respond.

item.title: German Regulator to Push Insurers to Fix Private Credit Failings
item.description: Germany’s top financial regulator will step up pressure on insurers to address shortcomings in private credit investments and issued a warning on an asset class increasingly marketed to retail clients.

item.title: EQT Makes Final Intertek Bid as Investors Push for Deal Talks
item.description: EQT AB has made a fourth and final bid to acquire Intertek Group Plc as the British product-testing company comes under increasing pressure from its investors to pursue a deal.

item.title: Billionaire Olayans, Tech Firms Look to Ignite Saudi IPO Revival
item.description: A raft of companies across sectors are pressing on with plans to list in Saudi Arabia, bolstered by gains in the benchmark since the regional war began.

item.title: Munich Re Says It Has Up to €2.5 Billion Private Credit Exposure
item.description: Munich Re said it has investments of as much as €2.5 billion ($2.9 billion) in private credit, an asset class that has been facing fund redemptions and scrutiny of underwriting standards.

item.title: Macron Announces €23 Billion Investment Deals at Africa Summit
item.description: The France-Africa summit has mobilized deals amounting to 23 billion euros ($27 billion), according to President Emmanuel Macron.

item.title: Calbee’s Potato Chip Bags Turn Black-and-White on Iran War Ink Shortage
item.description: An ink shortage caused by the Middle East conflict is forcing Japan’s biggest potato-chip maker to tone down its packaging, the latest sign that a widening raw-materials crunch is rippling through global markets.

item.title: EQT Raises Bid Again for UK Testing Firm Intertek
item.description: EQT AB raised its takeover offer for Intertek Group Plc to roughly £9.2 billion ($12.5 billion) as the private equity firm seeks to overcome repeated rejections from the UK product testing business.

item.title: UK 30-Year Yields Top 1998 High as Political Crisis Deepens
item.description: The UK bond market tumbled, driving long-term bond yields back to the highest in nearly three decades, as growing pressure on Prime Minister Keir Starmer to step down renewed concerns about the fragile state of Britain’s finances.

item.title: Yen Gains Abruptly Then Pulls Back During Bessent Visit to Japan
item.description: The yen strengthened sharply versus the dollar on Tuesday before falling back during a visit by US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent to Japan.

item.title: Amazon Kicks Off Debut Swiss Franc Bond Sale in Record Six Parts
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. has begun the sale of its first Swiss franc bonds as it looks to raise a record six-part deal in the currency.

item.title: Ethiopian in Talks With Airbus for Widebody, Regional Jet Order
item.description: Ethiopian Airlines Group is in early talks with Airbus SE to purchase regional and widebody aircraft as Africa’s largest carrier looks to expand its foothold on the continent and beyond.

item.title: Bain, LY Said to Offer to Buy Kakaku, Rivaling EQT Takeover Bid
item.description: Bain Capital and LY Corp. have submitted a joint offer to acquire Tokyo-listed Kakaku.com Inc., the $3.7 billion price comparison site operator fielding a takeover by EQT AB.

item.title: Bristol-Myers Inks Up to $15 Billion Licensing Deal With Hengrui
item.description: Bristol-Myers Squibb Co. inked a collaboration and licensing deal with Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals Co. that could be valued at as much as $15.2 billion, as the US drugmaker seeks to leverage China’s efficiency in early development.

item.title: Hitachi Said to Mull $640 Million Stake Sale in Machine Firm
item.description: Japanese industrial electronics maker Hitachi Ltd. is looking to offload about 8% of Hitachi Construction Machinery Co. shares in block trades, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Vodafone Sales Growth Beats as German Service Revenue Rises
item.description: Vodafone Group Plc reported organic revenue growth last quarter that beat analysts’ expectations as the mobile operator focuses on its biggest markets and Germany returned to growth.

item.title: Prosus Targets $3.6 Billion From Just Eat Sales in Europe Push
item.description: Prosus NV is targeting $3.6 billion in revenue from Just Eat Takeaway.com in a year as it looks to boost efficiency in its European food-delivery business at a time of rapid consolidation in the sector.

item.title: Paschi Beats on Profit as CEO Focuses on Mediobanca Integration
item.description: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA’s profit in the first quarter beat expectations, giving tailwind to Chief Executive Officer Luigi Lovaglio and his effort to integrate Mediobanca SpA.

item.title: UK Bonds and Pound Slump as Pressure on Starmer Builds
Motsepe Says Partnerships Boosting South Africa’s Mining Sector
item.description: South Africa has regained competitiveness in mining because of partnerships between the public and private sector to tackle regulatory issues and structural bottlenecks, according to billionaire Patrice Motsepe.

item.title: Uniper’s Trading Arm Returns to Profit as Gas Business Improves
item.description: Uniper SE’s trading division was back in profit in the first quarter as its gas division performed better and wasn’t impacted by supply disruptions related to the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Thyssenkrupp Trims Sales View While Keeping Profit Target Intact
item.description: Thyssenkrupp AG maintained its key earnings and cash-flow targets, while striking a more cautious note on sales because of heightened geopolitical uncertainty and its impact on international markets.

item.title: Taiwan Brokerages Seek About $1 Billion of Loans as Market Booms
item.description: Two of Taiwan’s biggest securities firms are seeking nearly $1 billion of loans to expand their businesses, according to people familiar with the matter, as investors pile into a booming stock market that has become the sixth largest in the world.
